title: Missense mutation in PRF1 gene, probably recessive

title: Familial Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis and Lymphoma
summary: Familial Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis and Lymphoma
detail: This mutation codes for a missense mutation in the perforin (PRF1) gene. It is suspected to be pathogenic. Most research suggests that it follows a recessive model, but in at least one case, a patient with heterozygous mutations in both PRF1 and FAS developed autoimmune lymphoproliferative syndrome and a large-B-cell lymphoma. In most cases, a single G allele may be benign, but more research is needed.
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.
